name: pause-work
description: Snapshot live session context into a durable .continue-here.md handoff and land a WIP commit so a future session can resume where this one left off

user-invocable: true

<!-- codex:plugin-root-preamble v1 — generated by scripts/codemod-plugin-root-preamble.js; edit the codemod, then re-run -->

> **Resolve the plugin root before running any script below.** A codex shell
> can inherit a stale, set-but-WRONG `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` from its launcher,
> so a bare `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-…}` fallback is NOT safe — validate the
> value against this skill's own SKILL.md first. Set `SKILL_MD` to this
> file's absolute path (codex lists it next to this skill in your skills
> list; under Claude Code the env var is always correct and the fallback
> branch never runs):
>
> ```bash
> SKILL_MD="/absolute/path/to/this/SKILL.md"   # from your skills list
> if [ -f "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT:-/nonexistent}/skills/pause-work/SKILL.md" ] &&
>    [ "${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/skills/pause-work/SKILL.md" -ef "$SKILL_MD" ]; then
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT"                         # validated — use as-is
> else
>   PLUGIN_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$SKILL_MD")/../.." && pwd)" # stale/unset — self-locate
> fi
> ```
>
> Wherever this skill writes `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}`, substitute the validated
> `$PLUGIN_ROOT` value (shells do NOT share variables between tool calls —
> re-resolve or inline the resolved path in each command).

# /pause-work - Session Handoff & WIP Checkpoint

Snapshot the **live** context of the current `/work` session into a durable
`.continue-here.md` handoff, validate it, and land a WIP commit — so a future
session (or a post-compaction one) can resume exactly where this one paused.

**Pause is context, not an unlock.** This skill NEVER reveals the session-guard
passphrase and NEVER calls `session-guard reveal` or `session-guard finish`. The
lock stays exactly as it was: a subsequent `Stop` during `implement` still
blocks with exit 2. See [Lock integrity](#lock-integrity--hard-rules) below.

## Usage

```
/pause-work PROJ-123        # Author the handoff, validate, WIP-commit for this ticket
/pause-work GH-315          # GitHub issue id
```

## What It Does

1. **Author `.continue-here.md` from live context** — YOU (the in-session agent)
   write the handoff narrative from what you know right now. Three L2 headings
   are REQUIRED:
   - `## Decisions made (and why)`
   - `## Blockers / warnings`
   - `## What was in flight`
2. **Validate the three sections** — the handoff is rejected before any commit
   if a required heading is missing (no skeleton commits).
3. **WIP commit** — a `chore(work): pause at <step> (task N/M) (#315)` commit is
   landed via `commit-and-push.js`, the only sanctioned commit path.

## Execution

### Step 1: Determine ticket and tasks folder

```bash
PLUGIN_ROOT="${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}"
node -e "const c = require('$PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/workflows/lib/config'); console.log(JSON.stringify({ TASKS_BASE: c.TASKS_BASE }))"
```

Use the argument as the folder name (ticket ID or slug). The handoff artifact
lives at `${TASKS_BASE}/${TICKET}/.continue-here.md`.

### Step 2: Author the handoff from live context

Write `${TASKS_BASE}/${TICKET}/.continue-here.md` yourself, filling all three
REQUIRED sections from what is true **right now** in this session:

```markdown
## Decisions made (and why)

<the concrete choices you made this session and the reasoning behind them>

## Blockers / warnings

<anything the next session must know before touching the code — failing tests,
half-applied migrations, flaky steps, review comments left open>

## What was in flight

<the exact task/step you were mid-way through, the next command to run, and any
partial edits not yet committed>
```

**No-secrets guidance.** NEVER write the session-guard passphrase, tokens, API
keys, or any credential into the handoff prose. The handoff is a plain artifact
that gets read back and injected into future prompts — treat it as public to the
project. If context references a secret, describe it by name/role, not value.

The agent authors the narrative — this skill and its hooks only prompt for and
validate the section presence; they never fabricate prose on your behalf.

### Step 3: Validate the authored handoff (reject skeletons)

Run the helper's validate guard BEFORE attempting any commit. It calls
`validateHandoffSections` and REFUSES (naming the missing headings) if a required
section is absent:

```bash
PLUGIN_ROOT="${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}"
node -e '
  const path = require("path");
  const { assertHandoffValid, resolveStepAndProgress, buildPauseCommitMessage } =
    require(path.join(process.env.CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT, "skills/pause-work/lib/pause-work.js"));
  const fs = require("fs");
  const ticket = process.argv[1];
  const file = path.join(process.env.TASKS_BASE, ticket, ".continue-here.md");
  assertHandoffValid(fs.readFileSync(file, "utf-8"));       // throws on a skeleton
  const { step, taskN, taskM } = resolveStepAndProgress(ticket);
  console.log(buildPauseCommitMessage({ step, taskN, taskM }));
' "${TICKET}"
```

If it throws, STOP: add the missing `.continue-here.md` section(s) and re-run.
Do not commit over an incomplete handoff.

### Step 4: Build the commit header

The header printed by Step 3 has the shape:

```
chore(work): pause at <step> (task N/M) (#315)
```

- `<step>` is resolved from `.work-state.json`'s current step.
- `N/M` is resolved from the `## Task N` progress in `tasks.md`.
- The type is the ALLOWED `chore(work)` type (NEVER `wip` — it is not in
  `ALLOWED_TYPES`) and the `(#315)` ticket ref makes the header pass the shared
  commit-message validator.

`buildPauseCommitMessage` in `skills/pause-work/lib/pause-work.js` owns this
shape — do not improvise the header by hand.

### Step 5: WIP commit via commit-and-push.js

Route the actual commit through `commit-and-push.js` — the ONLY sanctioned
commit path. Never shell out to a bespoke `git commit` for the pause:

```bash
PLUGIN_ROOT="${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}"
node "$PLUGIN_ROOT/scripts/workflows/work/scripts/commit-and-push.js" \
  --message "chore(work): pause at <step> (task N/M) (#315)"
```

Stage the authored `.continue-here.md` (and any in-flight work you intend to
carry over) before invoking `commit-and-push.js`.

### Step 6: Summary

After the commit lands:
- Confirm `.continue-here.md` exists with all three sections.
- Show the commit header that was used.
- Remind the user: resume by re-dispatching `/work ${TICKET}`. On resume the
  handoff is injected as **Continue Here (read FIRST)** ahead of the brief/spec/
  tasks required reading, then cleared after the first successful step advance.

## Lock integrity — hard rules

`/pause-work` is context capture, **not** an unlock. It MUST NOT:

- reveal or print the session-guard **passphrase** anywhere (stdout, stderr,
  the handoff prose, or the commit message);
- call `session-guard reveal` or `session-guard finish`;
- satisfy `blockStop` or otherwise release the session-guard lock.

Pause leaves the lock exactly as it found it. A `Stop` during `implement` after
`/pause-work` still blocks with exit 2 — pausing does not end the session or
bypass any workflow gate. If you need to end the session, that is a separate,
explicit action the user must take; pause never does it implicitly.

## Related

- `skills/pause-work/lib/pause-work.js` — `buildPauseCommitMessage`,
  `assertHandoffValid`, `resolveStepAndProgress`.
- `scripts/workflows/lib/handoff.js` — `validateHandoffSections`,
  `writeHandoff`, `readHandoff`, `deleteHandoff` and the required-section list.
- `scripts/workflows/work/scripts/commit-and-push.js` — the sanctioned commit route.
